questions.question: "J'adore" is a perfume from which fashion house?
Which two countries start with "T" and end in "… stan"?
What colour is the head, throat and abdomen of the rainbow lorikeet?
In which ABC TV series does Deborah Mailman play independent MP Alex Irving?
What is the outermost layer of the Earth called?
In computing, what does the "IP" in IP address stand for?
Which Impressionist painter said, "Perhaps flowers are the reason why I am an artist"?
According to Gen Z slang, what is a "finsta"?
questions.answer: A contraction of "fake" and "Insta", a finsta is a secondary Instagram account set up for private use

questions.question: The Matterhorn is on the border of which two countries?
Did Paul McCartney meet John Lennon at a church fête, football game or school dance?
Which fruit juice is used in a Cuba libre cocktail?
Name three of the five categories in the arts-based "Pentathlon of the Muses", a feature of the Olympic Games from 1912 to 1948.
questions.answer: Architecture, literature, music, painting and sculpture

questions.question: What is the brand name of the caramelised, spiced biscuits produced by Belgian company Lotus Bakeries?
In the 1967 film Cool Hand Luke, Paul Newman's character wins a bet by eating how many hard-boiled eggs?
Which Caribbean capital city's airport has the IATA code KIN?
What is the minimum age to take the official, supervised Mensa admission test in Australia: 10, 16 or 21?
True or false: buckwheat is a type of wheat?
In which 1667 epic poem does Satan declare, "Better to reign in Hell than serve in Heaven"?
Is a "stimpmeter" used in baseball, cricket or golf?
Which is the most commonly used noun in the English language: day, people or time?
What have I sneezed into if I've sneezed into my "chelidon"?
Is the character Ross Geller, played by David Schwimmer in the TV sitcom Friends, an archeologist, ornithologist or paleontologist?
Mycology is the study of … what?
A 2025 single by Melbourne-based singer-songwriter Ecca Vandal is Cruising to … what?
Thanatophobia is the persistent fear of … what?
